codeReview.summary: Web search tools (WebSearchTool, DomainSearchTool, FetchPageTool) are implemented as standalone Tool implementations with proper trait adherence, permission tiers, domain allowlists/blocklists, rate limiting infrastructure, and HTML content extraction. However, the tools are NOT registered anywhere - neither in ToolRegistry nor in any agent initialization. The feature spec explicitly requires 'Tools registered in ToolRegistry and available to ALL agents' and 'ResearcherAgent and other agents can invoke web search tools via tool_calls', but no registration code exists.

issues.description: Web search tools are implemented but never registered in ToolRegistry. The feature expectedBehavior states 'Tools registered in ToolRegistry and available to ALL agents', but there is no code that registers WebSearchTool, DomainSearchTool, or FetchPageTool in the registry. Search for 'register' in registry.rs shows only pattern-based registration, but web_search is absent.

ResearcherAgent exists with fields for tool_registry and can_build_from_llm_and_tools, but the agent does not have web search tools instantiated or registered in its with_tools builder. The expectedBehavior states 'ResearcherAgent and other agents can invoke web search tools via tool_calls', but ResearcherAgent has no access to web_search tools.

RateLimiter.is_allowed() is hardcoded to return true (rate limiting is a no-op). The struct has requests_per_minute, window_secs, and domain fields all marked with #[allow(dead_code)], indicating infrastructure exists but is not implemented. This is documented as intentional for MVP but represents incomplete functionality.

Worker transcript shows the session was interrupted/cancelled mid-execution ('Request cancelled by user'). Worker had to restart and continue. While the implementation appears complete, the interruption may have contributed to missing registration code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker implemented web search tools as new module but AGENTS.md has no documentation about how tools should be registered in ToolRegistry. The convention for registering tools is unclear - other tools (ReadFileTool, ShellTool, etc.) exist in tools.rs but their registration mechanism is not documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: web_search.rs module created but no register calls found anywhere. Compare to tools.rs where tools are defined but their registration path is unclear.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill 'Rust' was followed correctly - async_trait usage, proper Error handling with thiserror patterns via SwellError, use of Arc<dyn Tool>, chrono for dates, reqwest for HTTP. No deviations noted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true in handoff confirms skill adherence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No additional services were started. Web search uses DuckDuckGo HTML endpoint (https://html.duckduckgo.com/html/) which does not require authentication or API keys - no services.yaml entry needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows successful verification commands: cargo build --package swell-tools, cargo build --workspace, cargo test --package swell-tools web_search

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered the Tool trait and ToolRegistry structure by reading existing code (traits.rs, registry.rs). No online documentation was needed for implementation. The knowledge of how to register tools in the ToolRegistry should have been acquired from the codebase but was not applied.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker read traits.rs, registry.rs, tools.rs before implementing. However, registration in registry.rs requires explicit register() calls which were not added.

summary: The feature implementation has web search tools with proper Tool trait implementation, input schemas, behavioral hints, HTML cleaning, code block extraction, and provenance tracking. However, CRITICAL blocking issue: The tools are NOT registered in ToolRegistry and are NOT accessible to agents. The feature spec explicitly requires 'Tools registered in ToolRegistry and available to ALL agents' and 'ResearcherAgent and other agents can invoke web search tools via tool_calls' - neither requirement is met. The RateLimiter is also infrastructure-only (always returns true). The handoff verification commands passed because they only tested build and unit tests, not actual integration with ToolRegistry or agent tooling.
